Dakar Continental Information

Dakar Continental FIR is a Flight Information Region that spans multiple countries, namely Senegal (in full), Mauritania (in full), Ivory Coast (in full), Guinea-Bissau (partially), Mali (partially) and a slice of airspace from Burkina Faso.

Dakar Oceanic is part of the Dakar FIR, but operational purposes, we've split it from Dakar Continental.

Airspace Class Owner Limits Remarks
Dakar Continental G - GND - FL145 -
Dakar Continental G - FL145 - FL195 With the exception of ATS Routes
Dakar Continental A - FL195 - FL245 -
Dakar Continental UIR A - FL245 - UNLTD -
GOOC

SLOP

  • The use of SLOP in Dakar Continental is in compliance with provisions in ICAO Doc. 4444, between flight level FL280 and FL460.

GOOOC Dakar Continental | v0.1

Dakar Continental Sectors

* The Dakar Continental airspace is divided into the following sectors:

Abidjan

Position Callsign Frequency Remarks
DIII_CTR Abidjan Control 129.100 -
Airspace Class Owner Limits Remarks
Abidjan FIS G DIII_CTR GND - FL145 -
Abidjan FIS G DIII_CTR FL145 - FL195 Except for ATS Routes (Class A)
Abidjan FIS G DIII_CTR FL195 - FL245 -
Abidjan UTA A DIII_CTR FL245 - UNLTD -

Bamako

Position Callsign Frequency Remarks
GAAA_CTR Bamako Control 125.400 -
Airspace Class Owner Limits Remarks
Bamako FIS G GAAA_CTR GND - FL145 -
Bamako FIS G GAAA_CTR FL145 - FL195 Except for ATS Routes (Class A)
Bamako FIS G GAAA_CTR FL195 - FL245 -
Bamako UTA A GAAA_CTR FL245 - UNLTD -

Dakar

Position Callsign Frequency Remarks

Dakar Continental G GOOO_CTR GND - FL145 -
Dakar Continental G GOOO_CTR FL145 - FL195 With the exception of ATS Routes

Dakar Continental UIR A GOOO_CTR FL245 - UNLTD -

Nouakchott FIS G GQQQ_CTR FL145 - FL195 Except for ATS Routes (Class A)

Dakar Transition Table

  • Some airfields within SSA have published fixed Transition Levels. In this case, use the published transition level.
  • CAUTION – With a fixed Transition Level, it may mean less than 1,000 ft separation exists between aircraft operating at the Transition Altitude and Transition Level depending on the QNH.
  • Where a fixed transition level is not published, use the table below to determine the transition level.

Dynamic Transition Altitude Table

QNH 2000 3000 4000 5000 6000 7000 8000 9000 10000 14000
1060 - 1050 FL020 FL030 FL040 FL050 FL060 FL070 FL080 FL090 FL100 FL140
1049 - 1032 FL025 FL035 FL045 FL055 FL065 FL075 FL085 FL095 FL105 FL145
1031 - 1014 FL030 FL040 FL050 FL060 FL070 FL080 FL090 FL100 FL110 FL150
1013 - 995 FL035 FL045 FL055 FL065 FL075 FL085 FL095 FL105 FL115 FL155
  • Although the Transition Level may not be a ‘whole’ flight level. Commonly the ‘Lowest Useful Flight Level’ will be the nearest ‘whole’ flight level. E.g. FL85 would be FL90 (Always round up)
Airport Transition Altitude Transition Level (If Fixed)
GABS 3330 ft -
GAKD 2800 ft -
GAMB 3500 ft -
GBYD 2500 ft -
GOBD 3500 ft -
GOGG 2800 ft -
GGOV 3000 ft -
GOGS 3100 ft -
GOOY 2600 ft -
GOSS 2500 ft -
GQNO 3100 ft -
GQPP 2500 ft -
